body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Additional diagnostics for validating the land model
- snow cover fraction
- update integrated water to be in first 10cm, not first m, and convert to a mass per area using liquid water and ice density.
@gemini-code-assist ignore

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
	- Added a new diagnostic metric for snow cover fraction, now available in both detailed and summary outputs.
- **Refactor**
	- Updated diagnostic measurements to report integrated water mass over the top 10 cm instead of 1 m water content for improved accuracy.
	- Adjusted the short diagnostic configuration by removing an older variable.
	- Expanded the list of short names used in diagnostics to include "snowc".
	- Introduced a new alias for the Parameters module to streamline access within diagnostics.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
title: Add diagnostics

body:
This PR updates the leaderboard for ClimaLand and uses the last 12 months of either the observational and simulation data for computing RMSEs.

For example, if the observational data is only defined up to November 2013 and the simulation ended at the end of 2018, then the plot would look like this.
![et_bias_plot](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/38e5e5e0-d20e-43c7-834e-122abc843321)

Similarly, the plot of the RMSE and bias would look like this.
![global_rmse_and_bias_graphs](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/12d07f99-9cfb-4f1b-9329-776e5020b10c)

## TODO

- [x] Make a new release of ClimaAnalysis
- [x] Add check for whether all short names are valid in data sources. This is because there might be missing variables depending on whether we are using ILAMB or ERA5 data.


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
  - Updated artifact configuration to include a new dataset for ERA5 monthly averages (1979–2024) with direct download access.
  - Enhanced data handling by adding extra simulation variables and multi-source observational support.
  - Introduced monthly and seasonal leaderboard views with improved time windowing and visualization.
  - Adjusted simulation start dates based on run duration.
  - Provided a helper for easy retrieval of the ERA5 monthly averages dataset.
  - Updated project dependencies to the latest version of ClimaAnalysis (0.5.16).
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
title: Update leaderboard for longer run of snowy land
